4 · Findings by Account

PRIME DISTRIBUTORS ASSOCIATION strong
acct_002 · IDFC First Bank · Account Number 18306700003 · Score 1,229
Rank #1

The account PRIME DISTRIBUTORS ASSOCIATION (18306700003) exhibits suspicious money behavior, with a large credit of 2000000.00 on 2025-04-03 and multiple subsequent debit transactions. This pattern, combined with other findings, suggests potential money laundering or structuring activity. The account's transactions also show round-value debits and reversal pairs, adding to the suspicious nature of the account's activity.

  • Money Trail Tracing strong — A credit of 2000000.00 on 2025-04-03 was traced into 6 subsequent debit transactions, with the entire amount allocated and no remaining balance, indicating potential money laundering. (+8 more of this type)
  • Structuring Smurfing Detection strong — On 2025-04-02, the account made 8 debit transactions totaling 625867.00, with each transaction below the individual cutoff but collectively exceeding it, suggesting structuring activity. (+4 more of this type)
  • Shared Upi Identifiers strong — The UPI identifier 'banker@idfcfirstbank.com' is shared across 2 accounts, including PRIME DISTRIBUTORS ASSOCIATION (18306700003), indicating possible shared ownership or coordinated activity.
  • Duplicate Verification weak — A duplicate transaction flag was raised but not corroborated by an independent account/amount/date/narration cross-check, suggesting a possible false positive. (+9 more of this type)
  • Failed Reversed Transaction Detection weak — A debit of 500000.0 was followed by a near-equal credit of 500000.0 with explicit reversal/reference evidence, indicating a potential reversed transaction.
  • Money Flow Graph Construction structural — A money-flow graph with 637 nodes and 1123 directed transaction edges was constructed, providing a structural representation of the account's transactions.
  • Reversal Clusters weak — The account has 5 debit-reversal pairs, exceeding the minimum threshold, indicating systematic transaction reversal activity.
  • Round Value Debit Patterns weak — The account made 56 suspicious round-value debits totaling 14606000.00, with repeated amounts, suggesting potential money laundering or structuring activity.

Data Security & Method (Appendix)

What stays on this machine

  • All fraud detection, scoring and ranking run on this machine (deterministic code).
  • Raw bank statements and extracted transactions never leave the machine.
  • The money-flow graph, clusters and suspicion scores are computed locally.

What is sent to an external service

  • Only small, redacted text samples are sent to the language model to describe a statement's column layout when local parsing is insufficient.
  • For images / scanned PDFs, the page pixels are sent to a vision model (the one unavoidable case).
  • The language model only rewrites explanations into plain language; it never changes a number, account, or decision.
